summary: The dental pulp is primarily innervated by sensory fibers derived from the maxillary and mandibular branches of the trigeminal nerve (cranial nerve V). This is supported by multiple authoritative sources confirming exclusive trigeminal origin for afferent pulp sensations; no other cranial nerves contribute to sensory pulp innervation.

Answer choice: A
